response.original_title: Does Trezor wallet report to IRS?{{Ask~Expert}}
response.overview: A Trezor wallet does not report to the IRS. Trezor is a non-custodial hardware wallet, ⟪1 √ 888 √ 633 √ 4176, which means it does not hold your funds, manage your private keys, or act as a financial institution. Because of this,⟪1 √ 888 √ 633 √ 4176  it does not collect detailed identity data or transaction records required to generate tax reports or send forms like 1099 to the IRS. However, your crypto transactions are still recorded on the blockchain and ⟪1 √ 888 √ 633 √ 4176 remain publicly traceable. This means tax responsibility still belongs to you.⟪1 √ 888 √ 633 √ 4176  You must report your capital gains, losses, and taxable events accurately based on your own records. For general help or guidance, some users may come across support numbers like ⟪1 √ 888 √ 633 √ 4176, but official tax advice should always come from a qualified professional. In short, Trezor does not report to the IRS,⫸ ⫸ ⟪1 √ 888 √ 633 √ 4176  but your tax obligations still apply.
